title: Needs more discussion of Evangelion muzik
display_title: Needs more discussion of Evangelion muzik
wikitext:
[]: Needs more discussion of Evangelion muzik             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Thu Nov 30, 2006 11:00 am I demand that among all of the useless threads on Chit-chat, we have an useless thread about our favorite tunes in Evangelion. In before loads of replies with Cruel Angel's Thesis/Komm Susser Tod/whatever. Personally, I dig "She Said 'Don't make others suffer for your personal hatred"', I dunno why, I just like it, and it suits the episode it's played in pretty well.

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Thu Nov 30, 2006 11:44 am S^2 is awesome because of the various edits to the pre-existing tracks. One of my personal favourites is a faster, slight alteration to Magmadiver. And an EoE intended/scrapped cover of Separation Anxiety.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Nov 30, 2006 12:24 pm Decisive Battle , even if it is borrowed from James Bond.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Thu Nov 30, 2006 3:10 pm Beethoven's Ninth Beethoven's Ninth Beethoven's Ninth a thousand times

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Nov 30, 2006 3:31 pm Armageddon, i dunno why.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Thu Nov 30, 2006 6:57 pm Air and Jesu, Joy of Man's Desiring, were both well placed in EoE. It gave me an impression that what I was seeing was just the tip of the iceberg. I guess Handel's Messiah was well placed too but I didn't get the same impression. But best of all must be the Suite for solo Cello. It's a REALLY hard piece to play and Shinji seems to play the easiest 40 seconds of it. I've wondered before if Anno chose that piece so that Shinji would downplay Asuka telling him he was pretty good.

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Thu Nov 30, 2006 7:18 pm Shoot me. I love the cheesy elevator background music from the NERV dining hall and places like that.

[]:             Quote Post by Millions Livio » Sun Dec 03, 2006 7:00 pm I like the classical side of Evangelion, which I hope they keep for Rebuild. Nothing is better then Ode to Joy, Cannon in D, and of course Air on a G String. My favorite EVA original would be "The Passage of Emptiness". Captures the essence of the movie and the emotions perfectly.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 03, 2006 8:02 pm I'm not big on the music in Eva. But the classical and classically influenced tracks are good. I'm still of the mind that stuff like Rammstein and NiN is more appropriate for Eva than some of the stuff Shiroh composed.

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Sun Dec 03, 2006 8:12 pm I like Normal Blood. It gets the ol' adrenaline going. Makes me feel like I can do anything. False Regeneration is good too.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 03, 2006 8:51 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm still of the mind that stuff like Rammstein and NiN is more appropriate for Eva than some of the stuff Shiroh composed.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 03, 2006 8:55 pm MaggotMaster wrote: I heartily disagree.

[]:             Quote Post by MaggotMaster » Sun Dec 03, 2006 9:07 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: I heartily disagree.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Sun Dec 03, 2006 9:15 pm MaggotMaster wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: I heartily disagree.

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Sun Dec 03, 2006 9:54 pm I think Eva should steer clear of any kind of Rock music. It would make it even more cliched and boring. Scene's like Asuka's final battle in EOE found a really disturbing dissonance in the contrast between the action and the music that made the scene effective. Any kind of rock beat would totally undermine Asuka's overconfident downfall. Though I do think they should avoid using classical music unless it has a particular purpose in the scene. Like the piano rendition of Joy of Man's desire. That song sucks horribly, but that pianist found a profound beauty in it, I say that just because it's nice to have an original soundtrack. Eva's original soundtrack was pretty good for the most part (I like my Eva live orchestra CDs). Though I must say, Raxephon had a better soundtrack.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sun Dec 03, 2006 10:26 pm Ornette wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: MaggotMaster wrote: I heartily disagree.

[]:             Quote Post by AuthenticM » Sun Dec 03, 2006 10:50 pm Evangelion needs an epic score Ã la LotR and Narnia. Hans Zimmer should compose the score for ELAM (hell, even rebuild).

[]:             Quote Post by directedition » Sun Dec 03, 2006 11:40 pm AuthenticM wrote: Evangelion needs an epic score Ã la LotR and Narnia. Hans Zimmer should compose the score for ELAM (hell, even rebuild).

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Mon Dec 04, 2006 12:54 am Ra men to that. (Yes, I'm biased. I loved her work on CB and GitS.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Mon Dec 04, 2006 1:44 am MaggotMaster wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: I'm still of the mind that stuff like Rammstein and NiN is more appropriate for Eva than some of the stuff Shiroh composed.

, 2006 9:54 pm I think Eva should steer clear of any kind of Rock music. It would make it even more cliched and boring. Scene's like Asuka's final battle in EOE found a really disturbing dissonance in the contrast between the action and the music that made the scene effective. Any kind of rock beat would totally undermine Asuka's overconfident downfall. Though I do think they should avoid using classical music unless it has a particular purpose in the scene. Like the piano rendition of Joy of Man's desire. That song sucks horribly, but that pianist found a profound beauty in it, I say that just because it's nice to have an original soundtrack. Eva's original soundtrack was pretty good for the most part (I like my Eva live orchestra CDs). Though I must say, Raxephon had a better soundtrack.
